sweep_measurement_methodology.caveat: speed = step/elapsed includes Unsloth fused compile warmup (~10-15s). Short 50-step runs under-report steady-state by ~30%. Relative comparison across configs is valid; absolute MFU is pod- and run-length-dependent. Pod calibration scale r7_mfu/base_mfu=1.56 applied for r7-calibrated MFU column.
sweep_measurement_methodology.pod_calibration_note: This pod's b1_s1024_g8 measured 33.3 step/min (same config r7 pod measured 51.1). Most of the gap is compile overhead not yet amortized in 50 steps. Applied scale factor to other configs assumes compile overhead scales proportionally — valid within each (batch, seq) row, less valid across rows because seq=4096 compile takes longer.
sweep_measurement_methodology.vram_sampling: nvidia-smi query every 2s during run; peak may undershoot true max by <500 MiB

delta_vs_r7.tok_per_s_pct_raw_note: RAW comparison is misleading — r7 was measured at step 100 (post-compile steady state) on a different pod, sweep measured at step 50 (compile still amortizing) on r6 pod. The -26% is measurement-artifact not real regression.
delta_vs_r7.mfu_points_r7cal_note: Within-sweep relative: best config is +8.3 MFU points over worst (= r7's config b1_s1024_g8). This IS real — it reflects the batch/seq efficiency ranking even after pod+run-length normalization.

oom_configs.note: Peak 70.6 GB mostly forward-activation for seq=4096 micro-batch=8; OOM crash on attention kv-cache allocation during step 2. Safe upper bound: batch*seq <= 16384 tokens in micro-batch.

key_finding: effective_batch=8 via micro-batch is MORE efficient than via grad_accum. r7's config (b1_g8_s1024) ranks LAST on r7-calibrated MFU (62.6%) — converting grad_accum→micro_batch recovers ~8 MFU points. Seq scaling (1024→2048→4096) provides near-zero additional tok/s but costs VRAM.
pod_released_note: tmux sweep session killed, sweep_ckpt_* dirs removed, .sweep_active_task19 lock removed. r8 trainer /workspace/train_alm_14b_r8.py and task #24 ckpts (ckpt_alm_14b_r8, ckpt_alm_14b_r8_smoke) left untouched. GPU state 0 MiB verified.

coordination_with_task24.incident: Task #24 had tmux session 'r8-smoke' loading Unsloth model when sweep launched. Sweep grabbed GPU concurrently, forcing me to abort both. #24's smoke had not yet started training (stuck on 'Fetching 6 files' download phase — actually cached, just slow unsloth_zoo patching). Sweep then ran cleanly on free pod.
coordination_with_task24.state_left_for_24: r8 trainer, r8 launch script, both r8 ckpt dirs preserved. #24 can restart r8-smoke on same pod or take this sweep's findings and go straight to full r8 launch with optimal config.
coordination_with_task24.r2_pollution: Each sweep config uploaded a step_50 LoRA ckpt to r2:anima-models/alm14b/r8/step_50/ — the SAME r2 path gets overwritten 11 times. r8's real step_500/step_1000/... ckpts will still land cleanly but this sweep left a final step_50 adapter at r8/step_50/. NOT a concern for r8 full run which won't save at step 50.

recommendation_for_r8: CONFIG: batch=8 seq=1024 grad_accum=1 (effective_batch=8, same as r7). Expected +8 MFU points = ~70.9% vs r7's 62.6%. VRAM 42 GB well within budget. Alternative: batch=4 seq=1024 grad_accum=2 is essentially tied (70.3% MFU, 35.7 GB — lower VRAM, safer margin for any training-time spikes). Either choice is fine; b4_s1024_g2 is the conservative pick, b8_s1024_g1 is the aggressive pick. CAVEAT: if r8 wants seq>1024 for quality (e.g. longer holo window), b4_s2048_g2 is the best long-seq option at 69.6% MFU and 42.2 GB. Avoid b8_s4096 (OOM), avoid b1_* rows (all show effective-batch grad-accum inefficiency).
